---
title: "Overview"
---

# Stimulation reshapes the pain–symptom architecture in Parkinson disease

This book is the reproducible companion to the manuscript draft in
[`MANUSCRIPT_DRAFT.md`](MANUSCRIPT_DRAFT.md). It is auto-generated from
the analysis code in `R/` and `sprints/` and rebuilt by CI on every
commit to `main`.

## Reading order

1. [Methods](methods.qmd) — design, target trial emulation, statistical analysis.
2. [Results](results.qmd) — primary, secondary, exploratory findings.
3. [Sensitivity](sensitivity/index.qmd) — the nine post-hoc robustness sprints.
4. [Causal DAG](causal-dag.qmd) — dagitty source + interactive embed.
5. [STROBE](strobe.qmd), [TRIPOD-AI](tripod.qmd), [ROBINS-I](robins-i.qmd) checklists.
6. [Extending](extending.qmd) — porting the framework to other PD cohorts.

## Story in five bullets

1. **Primary safety floor**: DBS is non-inferior on the 4-year course of pain (TOST P < 10⁻¹², ±1-point margin).
2. **Network reorganization**: late-post NCT P = 0.050 — formal structural difference.
3. **Pain–motor decoupling**: bootstrap Δρ matched = −0.16 (95 % CI −0.60, +0.29).
4. **Not LEDD-mediated**: matched ACME P = 0.69 — stimulation-circuit mechanism.
5. **No genetic moderation**: PD-PRS / APOE / SAA / GBA × DBS all null.

**Elevator pitch**: *DBS doesn't change* how much *pain PD patients report; it changes* what their pain is linked to.*
